gwt-0001.name: a_new_query_call_is_issued_on_that_same
gwt-0001.given: a ClaudeSDKClient instance has completed one or more prior query() calls on an active connection
gwt-0001.when: a new query() call is issued on that same connected client
gwt-0001.then: the SDK appends the new prompt to the accumulated conversation history of the existing session, making all prior prompts and responses visible to the model in the new request

gwt-0002.name: the_model_processes_the_second_and_subse
gwt-0002.given: a ClaudeSDKClient instance is used for multiple sequential query() calls, each with a different session_id parameter
gwt-0002.when: the model processes the second and subsequent queries
gwt-0002.then: the model still has access to the full conversation history from prior queries regardless of the session_id values, because session_id is a tagging field and does not create an isolated context boundary

gwt-0003.name: the_model_processes_the_second_and_subse
gwt-0003.given: a ClaudeSDKClient instance is used for multiple sequential query() calls, each with max_turns=1
gwt-0003.when: the model processes the second and subsequent queries
gwt-0003.then: the model still has access to the full conversation history from prior queries, because max_turns limits tool-use round trips within a single invocation and does not reset the session context window

gwt-0004.name: the_async_generator_is_fully_consumed
gwt-0004.given: the standalone claude_agent_sdk.query() function is called with a prompt and ClaudeAgentOptions
gwt-0004.when: the async generator is fully consumed
gwt-0004.then: the call creates a brand-new session with no conversation history from any prior query() invocation, the model sees only the single prompt provided, and no state is retained after the generator is exhausted

gwt-0005.name: the_model_responds_to_extraction_b
gwt-0005.given: the standalone query() function is invoked for two different card extractions A and B, whether sequentially or concurrently
gwt-0005.when: the model responds to extraction B
gwt-0005.then: the model has no visibility into the prompt, partial response, or any content from extraction A, because each standalone query() call creates an independent session

gwt-0006.name: llm_fn_prompt_is_called
gwt-0006.then: the closure constructs a new ClaudeSDKClient, calls connect(), issues a single query(), collects the response via receive_response(), calls disconnect(), and returns the accumulated text — all within a single asyncio.run() call — so each invocation incurs a full connection setup and teardown

gwt-0007.name: sweep_remaining_iterates_all_pending_uui
gwt-0007.given: the crawl pipeline processes 1062 cards using the current _build_llm_fn() implementation
gwt-0007.then: 1062 independent connection setups and teardowns occur in series, with no network I/O overlap between extractions, making the sweep phase the dominant latency contributor

gwt-0008.name: extract_fn_skeleton_body_is_awaited
gwt-0008.given: _build_async_extract_fn() is used as the extract_fn factory
gwt-0008.then: the function calls the standalone claude_agent_sdk.query() with allowed_tools=[], system_prompt=_EXTRACT_SYSTEM_PROMPT, max_turns=1, and the constructed prompt; collects all TextBlock content from AssistantMessage events and the result field from any ResultMessage; joins the collected text; parses the JSON object from the raw output; and returns a FnRecord — without constructing or connecting a ClaudeSDKClient directly

gwt-0009.name: the_extraction_completes_without_raising
gwt-0009.given: extract_one() calls extract_fn(skeleton, body) on a successful first attempt
gwt-0009.when: the extraction completes without raising an exception
gwt-0009.then: extract_fn is called exactly once with no error_feedback argument, and the returned FnRecord is passed directly to store.upsert_record()

gwt-0010.name: extract_fn_is_called_for_attempt_n
gwt-0010.given: extract_one() is on retry attempt N (N >= 2) after extract_fn raised an exception on attempt N-1
gwt-0010.when: extract_fn is called for attempt N
gwt-0010.then: extract_fn receives the original skeleton and body unchanged, plus an error_feedback keyword argument equal to str(exception_from_attempt_N_minus_1), and the function appends a '## Previous Error' section containing only that single error string to the prompt text — no errors from attempts prior to N-1 are included

gwt-0011.name: extract_fn_skeleton_body_error_feedback
gwt-0011.given: extract_one() is on retry attempt N using standalone query() via _build_async_extract_fn()
gwt-0011.then: the standalone query() creates a fresh session containing only the current prompt (with the embedded error feedback section); no conversation history from the prior failed attempt is present in the context window

gwt-0012.name: sweep_remaining_processes_those_uuids
gwt-0012.given: CrawlStore.get_pending_uuids() returns a list of UUIDs representing records whose do_description is SKELETON_ONLY or EXTRACTION_FAILED
gwt-0012.then: each UUID's extraction depends only on that record's own skeleton and source body; no UUID's extraction result is an input to another UUID's extraction in the sweep phase, making all sweep records mutually independent

gwt-0013.name: both_runs_complete_without_error
gwt-0013.given: the same set of pending UUIDs is processed by _sweep_remaining() twice — once in the order returned by get_pending_uuids() and once in reverse order
gwt-0013.then: each UUID produces an identical FnRecord in both runs, confirming that processing order has no effect on extraction output

gwt-0014.name: the_asyncio_gather_call_is_awaited
gwt-0014.given: _sweep_remaining_async() is called with concurrency=N and a list of M pending UUIDs (M > N)
gwt-0014.then: at most N extract_one_async() coroutines are executing concurrently at any point in time, enforced by an asyncio.Semaphore(N); all M UUIDs are eventually processed; and the semaphore is acquired and released symmetrically for every UUID task

gwt-0015.name: asyncio_gather_return_exceptions_true_co
gwt-0015.given: _sweep_remaining_async() is running with concurrency=N and extraction task T raises an unhandled exception
gwt-0015.then: the exception from T is captured as a return value in the results list; all other in-flight and queued extraction tasks continue to completion without cancellation; the semaphore slot held by T is released

gwt-0016.name: two_concurrent_extract_one_async_corouti
gwt-0016.given: CrawlOrchestrator is constructed with an async_extract_fn and CrawlStore on a single asyncio event loop thread
gwt-0016.when: two concurrent extract_one_async() coroutines both complete and each calls store.upsert_record()
gwt-0016.then: the SQLite upsert calls execute sequentially without interleaving, because asyncio coroutines yield only at await points and store.upsert_record() is synchronous — eliminating the need for locks or check_same_thread=False on the SQLite connection

gwt-0017.name: process_extraction_result_discovers_thos
gwt-0017.given: CrawlOrchestrator._dfs_extract() is processing a node whose extraction result contains references to child nodes not yet visited
gwt-0017.then: the DFS traversal must await each child's extraction before discovering that child's own children; no DFS extraction may be parallelized because each result determines the next nodes to visit

gwt-0018.name: run_is_awaited
gwt-0018.given: CrawlOrchestrator.run() is the top-level entry point using the async-native design
gwt-0018.then: Phase 1 (_dfs_extract) executes all DFS nodes sequentially with sequential await calls in dependency order; Phase 2 (_sweep_remaining_async) executes all remaining pending UUIDs with asyncio.gather() and a semaphore; Phase 1 completes entirely before Phase 2 begins

gwt-0019.name: cmd_crawl_is_invoked
gwt-0019.given: cmd_crawl() is the CLI entry point for the async-native CrawlOrchestrator
gwt-0019.then: cmd_crawl() calls asyncio.run(orch.run()) exactly once, establishing a single event loop for the entire crawl operation; it does not call asyncio.run() per extraction and does not create nested event loops

gwt-0020.name: the_disconnect_coroutine_does_not_comple
gwt-0020.given: a ClaudeSDKClient disconnect() call is issued
gwt-0020.when: the disconnect() coroutine does not complete within 10 seconds
gwt-0020.then: the safe_disconnect() wrapper cancels the client's internal task group via client._tg.cancel_scope.cancel(), raises or swallows the resulting cancellation, and returns — ensuring the caller is never blocked indefinitely by a hung disconnect

gwt-0021.name: safe_disconnect_fires_the_10_second_time
gwt-0021.given: _sweep_remaining_async() is running with concurrency=N and one of the N in-flight extractions encounters a hung disconnect()
gwt-0021.when: safe_disconnect() fires the 10-second timeout and force-cancels the internal task group
gwt-0021.then: the semaphore slot held by that extraction is released within 10 seconds; other queued extraction tasks are unblocked and can acquire the slot; the total sweep is not permanently stalled by a single hung connection

gwt-0022.name: argparse_processes_the_command_line_argu
gwt-0022.given: the crawl CLI is invoked with a --concurrency flag
gwt-0022.then: the --concurrency value is parsed as an integer with a default of 10, validated, and passed through cmd_crawl() into CrawlOrchestrator.__init__() as the concurrency parameter, which is then forwarded to _sweep_remaining_async()

gwt-0023.name: cmd_crawl_constructs_the_crawlorchestrat
gwt-0023.given: the crawl CLI is invoked without a --concurrency flag
gwt-0023.then: CrawlOrchestrator receives concurrency=10 as the default and _sweep_remaining_async() uses asyncio.Semaphore(10)

gwt-0024.name: the_m_1_th_extraction_completes_and_the
gwt-0024.given: max_functions is set to limit M and _sweep_remaining_async() is running with concurrency=N where N > 1
gwt-0024.when: the (M+1)-th extraction completes and the total extracted count reaches M
gwt-0024.then: up to N-1 additional extractions that were already in-flight at the moment the limit was reached may also complete, causing the final extracted count to be between M and M+N-1 inclusive; this overshoot is an accepted tradeoff of the concurrent design

gwt-0025.name: the_inner_extract_fn_coroutine_is_about
gwt-0025.given: the CLAUDECODE environment variable is set in the process environment when _build_async_extract_fn() is called
gwt-0025.when: the inner extract_fn coroutine is about to call the standalone query()
gwt-0025.then: _build_async_extract_fn() removes CLAUDECODE from os.environ before importing claude_agent_sdk, ensuring the SDK does not operate in Claude Code mode during extraction queries

gwt-0026.name: the_anthropic_api_returns_a_rate_limit_e
gwt-0026.given: N concurrent extract_fn calls are in-flight via asyncio.gather() and each issues a standalone query() to the Anthropic API
gwt-0026.when: the Anthropic API returns a rate-limit error for one or more of the concurrent requests
gwt-0026.then: the rate-limit error surfaces as an exception in the affected extract_one_async() call; the retry logic in extract_one_async() handles it up to max_retries times using exponential backoff or re-raise; the semaphore slot is held until the retry sequence completes or max_retries is exhausted

gwt-0027.name: extract_bounded_checks_the_uuid_before_a
gwt-0027.given: a UUID appears in the list returned by get_pending_uuids() but is also present in _visited (already processed by DFS phase)
gwt-0027.when: _extract_bounded() checks the UUID before acquiring work
gwt-0027.then: the extraction is skipped without calling extract_one_async() and the semaphore slot is not consumed for that UUID, preventing duplicate processing of DFS-visited records during the sweep phase

gwt-0028.name: two_or_more_progress_messages_are_emitte
gwt-0028.given: _on_progress is called from multiple concurrent extract_one_async() coroutines to report progress to stderr
gwt-0028.when: two or more progress messages are emitted near-simultaneously
gwt-0028.then: each individual print() call to stderr is atomic at the OS level and produces a complete line without character-level interleaving with other print() calls, though line ordering across concurrent extractions is non-deterministic
